title: Critical assets are identified
description: The organization can name the systems, data sets, identities, and business services that must be recovered first after ransomware.
client_friendly_risk: If critical systems are not identified, recovery work may start in the wrong order and business downtime may last longer.
technical_risk: Missing asset and owner evidence makes it difficult to prioritize restore order, containment, dependencies, and validation after ransomware impact.

remediation_template.title: Create a critical asset and owner inventory for recovery
remediation_template.description: Document the systems, data sets, owners, dependencies, and recovery order that matter most to business operations.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This gives the recovery team a clear starting point when systems are unavailable.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Include identity providers, backup platforms, line-of-business applications, file stores, email, networking, and key SaaS systems.

title: Backups exist for critical systems
description: Critical systems and data have documented backup coverage and a defined backup schedule.
client_friendly_risk: If backups cannot be shown for critical systems, the organization may not be able to recover important data.
technical_risk: Unproven backup coverage leaves uncertainty around protected assets, job success, retention, RPO, and post-ransomware restore paths.

remediation_template.title: Prove backup coverage for critical systems
remediation_template.description: Collect current backup job configuration and show that critical systems are included on a defined schedule.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This proves the organization has recovery copies for the systems it depends on.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Map backup jobs to the critical asset list and note retention, frequency, last successful run, and failed-job handling.

title: Backup isolation or immutability is proven
description: At least one backup copy is protected from ordinary production or domain compromise through immutability, offline storage, or separated permissions.
client_friendly_risk: Reachable backups can be deleted or encrypted during ransomware, removing the main recovery option.
technical_risk: Without isolation proof, backup repositories may share the same credential and attack path exposure as production systems.

remediation_template.title: Prove immutable or isolated backup protection
remediation_template.description: Provide evidence that at least one backup copy is immutable, offline, or protected by separate administrative permissions.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This reduces the chance that ransomware can destroy every recovery copy.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Check immutability duration, delete permissions, backup console MFA, domain dependency, and break-glass access.

title: Restore test is proven
description: A recent restore test was completed and documented for at least one critical system or data set.
client_friendly_risk: Backups only matter if the organization can actually restore from them when ransomware disrupts operations.
technical_risk: No restore-test evidence leaves RTO, RPO, dependency, integrity, credential, and procedure assumptions unvalidated.

remediation_template.title: Run and document a controlled restore test
remediation_template.description: Restore a representative critical system or data set, record the result, and track any recovery blockers found.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This proves recovery works in practice, not only on paper.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Record restore source, target, credentials used, validation steps, elapsed time, data freshness, issues, and retest date.

title: Admin MFA is proven
description: Privileged and administrator accounts are protected by MFA, including MSP or remote administration paths where applicable.
client_friendly_risk: A stolen administrator password can let an attacker disable defenses, affect backups, or delay recovery.
technical_risk: Missing admin MFA proof increases credential compromise risk across identity, backup, remote access, and recovery tooling.

remediation_template.title: Collect proof of MFA for all privileged accounts
remediation_template.description: Show MFA enforcement for administrator, privileged, remote access, MSP, and emergency accounts.
evidence_needed: Privileged account list with MFA status
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This helps protect the accounts that could otherwise block or compromise recovery.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Review exclusions, legacy authentication, break-glass controls, service accounts, MSP accounts, and remote access paths.

title: Backup admin separation is proven
description: Backup administration is separated from ordinary production administration where practical, with least-privilege controls and review evidence.
client_friendly_risk: If the same compromised admin access controls production and backups, ransomware can disrupt recovery copies.
technical_risk: Shared administrative paths can allow lateral movement from production compromise into backup consoles and repositories.

remediation_template.title: Prove backup administration is separated from production administration
remediation_template.description: Document who can administer backups, how those accounts are separated, and when access was last reviewed.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This reduces the chance that one compromised account can damage both live systems and backups.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Review domain admin dependency, backup-console roles, MFA, just-in-time access, emergency access, and logging.

title: Endpoint detection or logging is present
description: The organization has evidence that endpoint alerts, centralized logs, suspicious sign-in monitoring, or mass file-change signals can be reviewed.
client_friendly_risk: If suspicious activity is not logged or monitored, ransomware may spread before anyone can act.
technical_risk: Weak logging and detection evidence limits incident scoping, containment decisions, root-cause analysis, and recovery validation.

remediation_template.title: Collect proof of endpoint detection and key logging coverage
remediation_template.description: Show which critical logs and endpoint alerts are collected, monitored, and assigned to an owner.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This gives the recovery team visibility into what happened and what may still be affected.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Prioritize identity, endpoint, server, backup, firewall, VPN, email, and file-storage telemetry.

title: Incident response contacts and playbook exist
description: A ransomware response playbook, contact list, roles, and escalation paths are documented and accessible during an outage.
client_friendly_risk: Without a simple playbook and contacts, the first hours of a ransomware incident can be confused and slow.
technical_risk: Unclear response ownership can delay containment, legal escalation, insurance coordination, recovery sequencing, and communications.

remediation_template.title: Create or collect a ransomware response playbook and contact list
remediation_template.description: Document roles, decision makers, technical owners, external contacts, escalation paths, and outage-safe contact methods.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This helps people know who acts, who decides, and who must be contacted during an incident.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Include containment, evidence preservation, backup protection, communication, legal, insurer, CERT/law enforcement, and restore approval steps.

title: Recovery priority list exists
description: Critical systems have an agreed restore order, practical RTO/RPO expectations, and known business owners.
client_friendly_risk: If the restore order is unclear, teams may recover low-priority systems before the services the business needs first.
technical_risk: Missing recovery priority evidence causes uncertainty around dependency order, acceptable data loss, validation, and business approval.

remediation_template.title: Document recovery priorities and business owners
remediation_template.description: Create a short list that states what must be restored first, who owns it, and the expected recovery targets.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This aligns recovery with the services that matter most to operations.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Tie the priority list to backup jobs, restore tests, identity dependencies, network dependencies, and validation steps.

title: External exposure review is documented
description: Internet-facing services and remote access paths have been reviewed through approved, non-invasive methods.
client_friendly_risk: Unknown public services or weak remote access can create avoidable ransomware entry points.
technical_risk: Unreviewed internet-facing services can hide vulnerable remote access, exposed management paths, and unmanaged cloud resources.

remediation_template.title: Document an authorized external exposure review
remediation_template.description: Collect a non-invasive, approved review of known public services, remote access paths, cloud exposure, and email security settings.
remediation_template.client_friendly_explanation: This helps prove that public access paths have been looked at without running unauthorized scanning.
remediation_template.technical_notes: Use imported reports, manual records, or approved inventory only. Do not probe live targets from this product version.
